title: clinopodium vulgare subsp. vulgare
intro: Clinopodium vulgare subsp. vulgare, also known as wild valerian or common valerian, is a subspecies of perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce stress, anxiety, and insomnia, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Clinopodium vulgare subsp. vulgare include reducing symptoms of menopause, improving sleep quality, and alleviating restlessness and irritability. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels.
